student_message: Yeah yeah I get it. Give me a question!
final_message: You are so ready! Let's start with a quick riddle. I am an invisible magic line where if you fold a shape right on top of me, both sides match perfectly, just like looking in a mirror! 🪞 Can you guess what kind of line I am? Once you figure that out, let's see if you can help Prem. He wants to cut out the letter V for Lali's birthday banner using the fold-and-cut method. How many of these lines of symmetry does the letter V have, which direction do they go, and how exactly should he fold the paper? 🦋

orchestrator_intent.goal: Provide a quiz question to test understanding and re-engage the student.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student claims to understand the concept and explicitly asked for a question. Since they are bored and looking away, a quiz will challenge and re-engage them.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
